name: Phenological Change Indicator (PCI)
abstract: Composite indicator of phenological change. PCI is published as a single CoverageJSON with a ParameterGroup of four parameters per pixel: trend (Theil-Sen slope of VAP day-of-year over the analysis window), anomaly (recent-vs-baseline mean difference), changepoint_year (Pettitt test) and changepoint_pvalue. Every parameter declares its observedProperty in the Bioclima ontology, so an analyst clicking a pixel in the viewer can navigate to the formal definition of each component. The bundle ships with code/compute.py and a worked example for Finland 2001-2018, and declares a formal policy alignment with KMGBF Target 8 and SDG 15.

sources.title: ▶ Open this layer in the BioClima interactive map viewer

description:
# Phenological Change Indicator (PCI)

Measures the rate of change of the start of the vegetation active period, in days/year.

## How it is computed

For every pixel, an ordinary-least-squares trend is fitted to the 18-year time series of VAP-DOY values, separately for coniferous and deciduous forests. The two trends are combined as a weighted mean (0.4 coniferous, 0.6 deciduous).

The implementation is in [`code/compute.py`](code/compute.py). The recipe in [`recipe.jsonld`](recipe.jsonld) declares all parameters semantically.

The CI workflow `recompute-indicators.yml` re-runs the script whenever an EBV input changes.

## Output

A CoverageJSON raster with the same spatial grid as the EBV inputs, single parameter `pci` in days/year. The Finland example produces ~13,600 valid pixels with trends ranging from -0.77 to +0.81 days/year.

examples.title: PCI output for Finland 2001-2018
examples.content: CoverageJSON output produced by code/compute.py from the two VAP EBVs.

annotatedSchema:
$schema: https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema
$id: https://maytetoscano.github.io/bblocks-bioclima/bblock/ogc.bioclima.indicator.pci/schema.yaml
title: "PCI \u2014 Phenological Change Indicator (4-parameter ParameterGroup)"
description: "Schema of a PCI CoverageJSON output. The PCI is a composite indicator\nencoded
  as a ParameterGroup of four per-pixel parameters:\n  - trend              (Theil-Sen
  slope, days/year)\n  - anomaly            (mean recent - mean baseline, days)\n
  \ - changepoint_year   (Pettitt argmax, year)\n  - changepoint_pvalue (Pettitt p-value
  approximation, dimensionless)\nConforms to ogc.bioclima.coveragejson.bioclima-coverage.\n"
type: object
required:
- type
- domain
- parameters
- ranges
- parameterGroups
properties:
  type:
    const: Coverage
  domain:
    type: object
  parameters:
    type: object
    required:
    - trend
    - anomaly
    - changepoint_year
    - changepoint_pvalue
    additionalProperties: false
    properties:
      trend:
        $ref: '#/$defs/Parameter'
      anomaly:
        $ref: '#/$defs/Parameter'
      changepoint_year:
        $ref: '#/$defs/Parameter'
      changepoint_pvalue:
        $ref: '#/$defs/Parameter'
  ranges:
    type: object
    required:
    - trend
    - anomaly
    - changepoint_year
    - changepoint_pvalue
  parameterGroups:
    type: array
    minItems: 1
    contains:
      type: object
      required:
      - id
      - type
      - members
      properties:
        id:
          const: https://maytetoscano.github.io/bblocks-bioclima/ontology/indicator/pci
        type:
          const: ParameterGroup
        members:
          type: array
          minItems: 4
          uniqueItems: true
          items:
            enum:
            - trend
            - anomaly
            - changepoint_year
            - changepoint_pvalue
  metadata:
    type: object
$defs:
  Parameter:
    type: object
    required:
    - type
    - observedProperty
    - unit
    properties:
      type:
        const: Parameter
      observedProperty:
        type: object
        required:
        - id
        - label
        properties:
          id:
            type: string
            format: iri
            pattern: ^https://maytetoscano\.github\.io/bblocks-bioclima/ontology/indicator/(pci|pci-.+)$
          label:
            type: object
            required:
            - en
      unit:
        type: object
        required:
        - id
        - symbol
        properties:
          id:
            type: string
            format: iri
          symbol:
            type: string
